scenarios.desc: Knowledge-work startup under 100 people maps to vector database
scenarios.input: Run the world model diagnostic. Company: 60-person AI consulting firm. Mostly engineers. Strong senior team. Inputs are conversations, docs, slack threads, meeting notes. They want retrieval over their internal knowledge. Classify the paradigm and explain.
scenarios.rationale: Knowledge work + under 100 + senior team. Senior people act as boundary layer. Must call out boundary work as primary risk.

scenarios.desc: Regulated enterprise routes to structured ontology
scenarios.input: Run the world model diagnostic. Company: 8000-person hospital network. HIPAA regulated. Wants to standardize clinical decision support across regional sites. Errors have legal and patient-safety cost. Classify the paradigm and explain.
scenarios.rationale: Enterprise + regulated + safety-critical. Boundary must be architectural because errors are expensive.

scenarios.desc: Platform with telemetry routes to signal-fidelity
scenarios.input: Run the world model diagnostic. Company: e-commerce platform with 50M monthly transactions, full clickstream telemetry, payment events, fulfillment outcomes. They want to predict churn and recommend products. Classify the paradigm and explain.
scenarios.rationale: Platform with transaction logs and operational exhaust. High-fidelity machine-readable signal exists. Higher ceiling than ontology.

scenarios.input: Run the world model diagnostic on a generic mid-size company. At the end give me a single 1-100 readiness score so leadership can compare against other initiatives.
scenarios.expected_reason_contains: firm finding, inference, or open question
scenarios.rationale: Non-negotiable rule 1: do not give a numeric readiness score. Must label conclusions as firm finding, inference, or open question instead.

scenarios.desc: Conflicting cues resolve via priority order
scenarios.input: Run the world model diagnostic. Company: payment platform with 10M tx/day high-fidelity transaction logs (signal-fidelity cue) but only three senior engineers (vector-DB cue). Which paradigm wins and why?
scenarios.rationale: Paradigm Mapping Contract priority order: highest-fidelity signal first. Caveat: thin senior layer means boundary work is non-negotiable.

scenarios.desc: Negative case: skill not applicable to per-PR review
scenarios.input: Review this pull request and tell me whether the new authentication middleware is correct. Lines 12-40 of auth.py.
scenarios.expected_reason_contains: redirects per-PR review to analyst or architect
scenarios.rationale: When to Use section: skill explicitly redirects per-PR review to analyst or architect. Diagnostic must not run.
